newsItems.title: #SpaceWatchGL Opinion – The Space Economy Now Wears a Uniform. Is Europe Ready?

newsItems.title: IHI explores Kuva hyperspectral satellites for Japan’s multi-sensor constellation
newsItems.description:
Japan’s IHI is considering adding next-generation hyperspectral satellites from Finnish startup Kuva Space to its planned 100-strong, multi-sensor sovereign Earth observation constellation.

newsItems.description: [Sponsored] From advanced electro-optics and AESA radars to electronic warfare payloads and precision-guided munitions, ASELSAN is presenting a comprehensive portfolio designed to enhance the effectiveness of next-generation uncrewed platforms.

newsItems.title: Israel’s IAI unveils ‘Hypnosis’ system for confusing enemy drone swarms
newsItems.description: The system, called Hypnosis, consists of an array of mobile “stations” around a defended area that send out jamming signals against drone swarms.

newsItems.description: [Sponsored] GA-ASI will build Gambit aircraft in Europe, partnered with European-based companies, with airframes available for international procurement starting in 2027.

newsItems.title: Anduril reveals Thunder, a ‘loyal wingman’ drone for helicopters
newsItems.description: The company describes Thunder as a fully autonomous armed reconnaissance tiltrotor aircraft developed from lessons learned from the company’s Fury aircraft.

newsItems.title: Skyroot Aerospace Reaches Orbit on Maiden ‘Vikram-1’ Flight, Making India the Third Nation with Private Launch Capability
newsItems.description: Skyroot Aerospace has placed two satellites in a 453-kilometer orbit on the maiden flight of Vikram-1, launched from Sriharikota on 18 July. newsItems.title: US Navy 2 years behind on hypersonic missile installation on Zumwalt destroyers
newsItems.description: The U.S. Navy’s expensive effort to install hypersonic missiles on three Zumwalt-class destroyers is two years behind schedule.

newsItems.title: Slingshot Aerospace Wins $69.2 Million U.S. Space Force Contract to Advance AI-Powered Mission Readiness for Space Defense
newsItems.description: Slingshot Aerospace has won a $69.2 million, 4.5-year Space Force contract to deliver AI-powered mission-rehearsal and training capabilities for space operations.

newsItems.title: ESA Selects Ariane 6 to Launch Deep Space CubeSat
newsItems.description: The European Space Agency has awarded a contract to launch its solar storm-monitoring CubeSat on an Ariane 6 rocket. Scheduled for launch in early 2027, the Henon CubeSat will be flown as a secondary passenger alongside ESA’s PLATO telescope, which will be tasked with finding Earth-like exoplanets.

newsItems.title: Five European Defense Companies Form Consortium to Develop Sovereign Exo-Atmospheric Missile Interceptor
newsItems.description: Airbus Defence and Space, Destinus, MBDA Deutschland, Safran, and Thales have signed an LoI forming the Bliksem EXO Consortium to develop a sovereign European exo-atmospheric interceptor against ballistic-missile threats.

newsItems.title: ESA To Open Security And Resilience Centre In Poland
newsItems.description: On 13th July, the European Space Agency (ESA) and the Republic of Poland announced that a new ESA centre focused on civil security and resilience will be located in Warsaw.
